macro_context.headline: PPI surged 1.4% MoM -- hottest since March 2022 -- but S&P 500 hit new ATH as tech and AI momentum overrode inflation fears ahead of Trump-Xi summit.

sections.past: EUR/USD slipped to 1.1712 as the dollar caught a bid on a scorching PPI print -- wholesale inflation jumped 1.4% MoM, the hottest since March 2022.
sections.why: The PPI surprise pushes back Fed cut expectations, but the real test is whether the Trump-Xi summit in South Korea produces any trade de-escalation that could undercut the greenback.
sections.watch: Trump-Xi face-to-face tomorrow -- any tariff rollback language sends DXY lower and EUR higher; a breakdown re-opens 1.1650.

headlines.title: US dollar rises as inflation jumps; Trump-Xi talks begin
The Euro’s Next Move Will Make or Break FIDI’s 2026 Returns
Dollar Slides on Stock Strength and Weak Consumer Sentiment

sections.past: Cable dropped to 1.3517, giving back 75 pips as the dollar firmed post-PPI. UK had no major data to counter the move.
sections.why: Sterling is caught between a hawkish BoE stance and broad dollar strength; the pair is trading on US macro, not UK fundamentals right now.
sections.watch: UK employment data due Thursday -- a soft labor market reading could accelerate GBP weakness toward 1.3450.

headlines.title: U.K. PM Starmer Is in Danger. Why the Timing of His Exit Matters for Markets.
U.K.’s Starmer Fights for Job as Leadership Crisis Spills Into Markets
Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter

sections.past: Dollar-yen pushed to 157.85, gaining nearly 40 pips as US yields climbed on the hot PPI. The 10Y hit 4.48%.
sections.why: Rising US yields widen the rate differential further, but the pair is nearing levels where BoJ verbal intervention historically begins -- watch for Tokyo commentary.
sections.watch: Japan GDP prelim due Thursday; a weak print could paradoxically strengthen yen if it triggers BoJ caution on further tightening.

sections.past: Aussie edged up to 0.7266, shrugging off dollar strength as copper and iron ore held firm on China stimulus hopes ahead of the Trump-Xi meeting.
sections.why: AUD is trading as a China proxy -- any positive trade signals from the summit would disproportionately benefit the Aussie given Australia's commodity export exposure.
sections.watch: China industrial production and retail sales data due Friday -- those numbers will confirm or deny the recovery narrative supporting AUD.

headlines.title: US dollar rises as inflation jumps; Trump-Xi talks begin
The Dollar is Now Lower Than When the Iran War Began
Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds

sections.past: Gold held near $4,708, essentially flat despite a surging dollar and rising yields -- a notable show of resilience.
sections.why: The inflation backdrop is gold's friend even as yields rise; real rates matter more than nominal, and PPI running hot keeps the stagflation hedge bid alive.
sections.watch: CPI follow-through tomorrow -- if core comes in above 3.5%, gold likely breaks $4,750; a downside surprise could see profit-taking to $4,650.

headlines.title: If You Want To Know Where Bitcoin Is Headed Next, Watch The Price Of Copper
i-80 Gold Reports Q1 2026 Results: Full Earnings Call Transcript
Semiconductor ETFs are booming, but they're not the only ones

sections.past: Silver surged nearly 12% to $89.80, massively outperforming gold. Industrial demand narrative is accelerating with AI data center buildout.
sections.why: The gold-silver ratio is compressing fast -- silver's dual role as industrial metal and monetary asset is finally being repriced as semiconductor and solar demand projections get revised up.
sections.watch: Watch the $90 psychological level -- a clean break opens the path to triple digits; rejection here could trigger a sharp mean-reversion given how extended the move is.

sections.past: WTI crude jumped 6.8% to $101.89 as OPEC cut its 2026 demand growth forecast but geopolitical risk premium surged on Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz concerns.
sections.why: The supply story dominates demand worries -- Strait of Hormuz shipping lane fears are adding $5-8 of risk premium, and any escalation takes oil toward $110.
sections.watch: EIA crude inventories Wednesday -- a large draw would confirm tight supply; Trump-Xi summit could ease Iran tensions if discussed, capping upside.

headlines.title: Exchange-Traded Funds, US Equities Mixed After Midday
Higher Oil Prices Fuel Long-Term Outlook For This Energy Stock
OPEC Lowers 2026 Global Oil Demand Growth Forecast, Lifts 2027 Outlook

sections.past: S&P 500 rose 1.1% to a new intraday all-time high at 7,447 as tech enthusiasm overwhelmed a scorching PPI print that showed wholesale inflation at 6% YoY.
sections.why: The market is choosing to look through inflation to the AI capex cycle -- Nvidia, Micron, and semis leading tells you this is a growth bet, not a macro bet.
sections.watch: CPI tomorrow is the make-or-break -- if core stays elevated, the rates repricing could finally dent equity multiples; but tech momentum may power through.

headlines.title: Sony Music Publishing Lands Iconic Song Catalog In Major Blackstone Deal
3 Reasons MAS is Risky and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
3 Reasons to Sell SYY and 1 Stock to Buy Instead

sections.past: Nasdaq 100 gained 2.7% to 29,372 as semiconductor stocks exploded higher -- SMH ETF up 2%, with Nvidia leading the charge on AI demand optimism.
sections.why: The second wave of AI infrastructure spend is being priced in; Alibaba's AI revenue boom despite margin compression shows the TAM is expanding globally.
sections.watch: Nvidia's price action at $230 resistance is key -- a breakout likely pulls the entire index higher; CPI miss could trigger a rotation out of growth.

headlines.title: AI boom might actually be tame compared to the dot-com bubble
Stocks Nosedive on Hot PPI Inflation Report

sections.past: Dow dropped 0.6% to 49,623, lagging badly as the index's old-economy tilt missed the tech rally. Value stocks sold off on inflation fears.
sections.why: Hot PPI hits Dow components hardest -- industrials, financials, and consumer staples face margin pressure from input cost inflation that tech largely avoids.
sections.watch: Ford and GM rallied on EV optimism, but the broader Dow needs CPI relief tomorrow to stop the rotation into Nasdaq names.

headlines.title: Exchange-Traded Funds, US Equities Mixed After Midday
US Equity Indexes Mixed as Hot Producer Prices Push Dow Jones Industrial Average Lower
S&P 500, Nasdaq Head Back to Record Levels

sections.past: Bitcoin pulled back 1.7% to $79,309 despite broad risk-on in equities. Crypto is diverging from its usual tech-beta behavior.
sections.why: The correlation break is telling -- BTC may be repricing as an inflation hedge rather than a risk asset, but conviction is low with the Trump-Xi summit adding uncertainty.
sections.watch: The $80K level is pivotal -- losing it opens $75K; reclaiming it with volume would signal the dip is buyable. Watch ETF flow data for institutional positioning.

sections.past: Ethereum dropped 3.1% to $2,255, underperforming Bitcoin. The ETH/BTC ratio continues to compress as capital rotates to BTC dominance plays.
sections.why: Layer-2 scaling is cannibalizing mainnet fee revenue, making the ETH investment case harder to articulate while BTC benefits from simpler store-of-value narrative.
sections.watch: ETH needs to hold $2,200 support or risk accelerating to $2,100. Any positive Pectra upgrade news could reverse the trend.

sections.past: Nvidia surged 9% to $226.60, its best day in weeks, as AI infrastructure demand signals intensified. Semiconductor ETFs hit record highs.
sections.why: The second wave AI thesis is going mainstream -- data center capex projections keep getting revised up, and Nvidia remains the picks-and-shovels monopoly play.
sections.watch: Approaching the $230 resistance level from pre-correction highs. A breakout on volume confirms the trend; earnings guidance season for cloud customers will be the next catalyst.

headlines.title: Why the Second Wave of AI Will Mint More Millionaires Than the First -- and the Stocks to Own
Alibaba's AI Business Is Booming, But Its Profits Basically Disappeared
Sector Update: Tech

sections.past: Tesla rocketed 12.3% to $447.89, leading the S&P 500 as EV stocks broadly rallied. Lucid and NIO also jumped 9% and 7% respectively.
sections.why: The EV sector is catching a bid from two angles -- Ford's strong results legitimize the space, and Trump's China trip raises hopes for reduced EV tariff barriers.
sections.watch: Tesla China sales data this week is the near-term catalyst. The stock needs to hold above $430 to confirm the breakout from its recent range.

headlines.title: Lucid Jumps 9%, NIO Rallies 7%, Tesla Rises 4% as EV Stocks Get Charged Up
Why Ford Stock Soared Today
Ford Stock Is Today’s Best Performer in S&P 500. Other Car Makers Also Move Higher.

sections.past: Apple climbed 4.4% to $300.21, crossing the $300 level for the first time as the broader tech rally lifted all boats.
sections.why: The Trump-Xi summit is particularly relevant for Apple given its massive China manufacturing exposure -- any tariff de-escalation is a direct margin tailwind.
sections.watch: The $300 round number is now support -- holding above it would be bullish. WWDC AI announcements in June are the next company-specific catalyst.

headlines.title: Soaring iPhone Sales Have Supercharged This Under-the-Radar Stock That's Outperforming Apple. Buy It Before It Soars Another 59%
Trump Lands In China With $16 Trillion Of CEO Power — And One Big Ask For Xi
Is Himax Technologies, Inc. (HIMX) A Good Stock To Buy Now?

sections.past: Microsoft fell 2.4% to $404, bucking the tech rally. The Musk-OpenAI trial testimony from CEO Nadella added uncertainty around the AI partnership structure.
sections.why: The OpenAI revenue-sharing cap discussion is creating overhang -- if OpenAI restructures its profit-sharing, Microsoft's AI economics could change materially.
sections.watch: Watch for trial developments this week. A resolution or clarity on the OpenAI structure removes a key uncertainty; continued drama could push MSFT below $400 support.

sections.past: Meta edged up 0.3% to $614.98, underperforming the Nasdaq. AI-related layoff concerns and internal tensions dampened sentiment.
sections.why: Meta's AI pivot is generating revenue growth but the workforce restructuring signals suggest margins are under more pressure than the headline numbers show.
sections.watch: The $620 resistance has capped three rallies this month. A break above on strong volume would target $650; failure keeps the stock range-bound.

headlines.title: Why Nvidia Bulls Are Suddenly Watching Nebius Ahead Of NVDA Earnings

sections.past: Alphabet gained 0.8% to $401.38, modestly outperforming. Anthropic partnership news provided a small boost to the AI narrative.
sections.why: Google's cloud and AI positioning keeps improving, but antitrust overhang remains the structural discount that limits multiple expansion.
sections.watch: Google I/O announcements are the upcoming catalyst. Any Gemini product breakthroughs could re-rate the stock; antitrust ruling timeline is the key risk to monitor.

headlines.title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) Poised to Usurp Nvidia as Valuable Company on AI Boom
3 Top Stocks That Look Like Bargains Today
Assessing Alphabet (GOOGL) After A Powerful Share Price Run And Mixed Valuation Signals

sections.past: Amazon fell 2.1% to $269.36 as the hot PPI print raised concerns about consumer spending power. AWS growth narrative took a backseat.
sections.why: Amazon is caught between its AI/cloud growth story and its consumer retail exposure -- rising wholesale prices signal margin pressure on the e-commerce side.
sections.watch: The $265 support level from April's consolidation is the line in the sand. Watch for any retail sales data Thursday to gauge consumer health.
